metadesc: The analyst-influencer model is reshaping esports broadcasting as TikTok turns casters into brands. Learn how analysts grow reach and audiences in 2025.
From player to caster: discover how Malaysian pros and coaches build new careers as MPL shoutcasters, turning game knowledge into a lasting esports future.
Masta Studios leads Southeast Asia's media production shift, blending creativity, talent and technology to power the region's next generation of content.
Multilingual shoutcasters are the future of Malaysian esports, connecting cultures and expanding audiences. See how language diversity drives broadcasting.
Meet HusKKiee, Malaysia's high-energy MPL caster known for versatile delivery across MLBB, Free Fire and PUBG Mobile, plus M-Series world championships.
Meet Sdafhaa, Malaysia's energetic hybrid MPL shoutcaster and Twitch streamer blending commentary with content across major world championships.
Meet ReezPiuPiu, MPL Malaysia's fast-paced female caster whose rapid-fire commentary brings energy and precision while shaping esports' future.
Meet Artha, the former Team SMG pro player and coach now ranked among MPL Malaysia's most respected casters for his aggressive, fast-paced delivery.
Meet GideonQ, MPL Malaysia's underrated English caster covering every M-Series world championship since M1 with trusted fast-paced play-by-play.
Meet Sunfrost, the MLBB coach turned hype caster of MPL Malaysia, blending energetic delivery with pro-level strategy across major championships.
Meet Adeen, MPL Malaysia's playful analyst whose mix of humor and sharp game knowledge makes him a fan favorite across major MLBB championships.
Meet Mars, MPL Malaysia's Talent of the Year, a multi-award shoutcaster and desk host recognized as one of the nation's most respected esports voices.
Meet Laphel, Malaysia's analyst-influencer redefining MPL commentary by turning complex Mobile Legends strategy into engaging, award-winning content.
Meet Rose, Malaysia's trilingual queen of esports broadcasting, hosting MPL in Bahasa Malaysia, English and Tagalog across M-Series world championships.
